title: goeppertia pumila
intro: Goeppertia pumila, also known as the Dwarf Calathea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, primarily as an anti-inflammatory agent to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant's leaves have been used in herbal preparations to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems, due to their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In addition to its medicinal uses, Goeppertia pumila is also valued for its air-purifying qualities, making it a popular choice for indoor spaces.
